behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model-output quality from real speech-to-text diarization (LLM-DEP yes for AI/ML output content). A stubbed transcript with perfect labels would hide diarization mistakes (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets one model-output property (speaker labels), not multi-agentic handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert transcript turns include stable speaker labels that map to known attendees across speaker changes.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is parser logic on transcript text/schema, with no required real I/O or model judgment (MOCK-MISS: local function/object boundary). Rejected Integration because description does not require cross-module state handoff; it specifies extraction logic itself.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript fixtures containing explicit assignment utterances, assert parser returns normalized records with correct assignee and task text fields.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real summarizer model decisions about coverage and attribution (LLM-DEP yes). Deterministic fixtures would always pass and miss omission errors (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because behavior text checks one output-quality criterion in the live summary, not multiple agentic stage-to-stage correctness assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript inputs with known per-attendee assignments, call the real summarization model, and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one corresponding action item.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is routing logic after decisions are already identified; this is deterministic schema mapping within local code (MOCK-MISS points to single-function boundary). Rejected Agentic because phrase "identified decisions" disqualifies dependence on model judgment for this specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed pre-identified decision objects into the mapper and assert output places them only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This validates decision recall/placement quality from real model output (LLM-DEP yes). Mocked outputs would hide failures where the model misses or misplaces decisions (MOCK-HIDE). Rejected Workflow because this behavior asserts a single end-result quality property, not sequential multi-agentic stage correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript samples with known decisions through the real summarization step and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (segment transcribe/summarize then merge) and checks coherence across chained outputs (MULTI-STEP yes). Integration would miss failures from real model-to-model handoff across segments (INTEGRATION-BRAKE d). Rejected Agentic because behavior explicitly includes sequential splitting, processing order, and merge across segments.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute recording, execute full segmented processing with real model calls per segment, then assert segment order handling, merged output completeness, and cross-segment coherence in final structured summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly full pipeline with real recording and multiple agentic stages end-to-end (MULTI-STEP yes). System alone is insufficient because success depends on chained model decisions and stage handoffs, not only infrastructure. Rejected Agentic because text requires the entire multi-stage pipeline, not one model step.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end job on a real meeting file through production-like upload, transcription, summarization, and output delivery, asserting automatic completion and valid final structured schema.
